body:
Lily,

Following up on the morning stack question from last week. Still running the same protocol — Chemex black, then the smoothie (whey, banana, spinach, almond butter, creatine) before I head out for the 5:15 at CrossFit Buckhead. Vitamin D 4000 IU and fish oil right after. No changes.

What I need to know: you mentioned there might be a better sequencing for the D and fish oil relative to food. I'm taking both with the smoothie right now. Is that optimal or am I leaving absorption on the table?

Also flagged by a patient that creatine timing matters more than I thought. I've always just thrown it in the smoothie pre-training. Worth reconsidering?

Keep it short — I've got a full clinic day. Appreciate you.

Brian

Hi Brian,

Good news — you're mostly dialed in. Taking vitamin D with a fat-containing meal significantly boosts absorption, and your smoothie with almond butter checks that box perfectly. Fish oil alongside it is just as solid. No changes needed there.

On creatine timing: the research is actually pretty forgiving. Pre-workout is fine, especially for someone training at your intensity level. The 'post-workout is better' argument exists but the difference is marginal. Consistency matters far more than the clock.

One small flag — if you're ever running low on sleep or feel like recovery is lagging, splitting the fish oil (half AM, half PM with dinner) can help some people. Not a must, just an option to have in your back pocket.

You're running a tight protocol. Keep it.

Lily

Hey Lily,

Quick one. Hit my Chemex at 4:40 this morning, was out the door on time, showed up for the 5:15 at Buckhead — and the front door reader wouldn't take my card. Stood outside about ten minutes before another member got it open with a backup code. That's the second time in three weeks, same window.

Can you look into the early-morning access issue on the keycard system? I'm there Monday through Thursday minimum, sometimes Friday depending on the training block. If there's a maintenance window scheduled, just tell me the dates so I can adjust. I don't need the full story — just the fix and the timeline.

Appreciate it.
— Dad (Brian Hall, Member #4421)

Hi Dad,

Thanks for reaching out — and sorry you got stuck outside before a 5:15! I pulled your account and your access credentials are active and valid, so this is definitely on our end, not yours.

I escalated to our facilities team this morning. Turns out the door reader firmware update pushed last Thursday has an intermittent failure on the 4:30–5:45 AM slot specifically. They're deploying the rollback patch tonight, so you should be clear by tomorrow's class. Backup code in the meantime: 6612# on the side keypad — works even when the reader is out.

I'll text you once facilities confirms the patch is live. Don't let a sticky door reader mess up your clean day!

— Lily | CrossFit Buckhead Member Support

Lily,

Good. That's exactly what I needed — clean answer, fast. Backup code is saved.

Thursday is a heavy clean & jerk day, so that patch better hold. I'll test the reader Wednesday morning and use the code if it acts up. If it's still glitching after the rollback, I'll loop back to you directly.

Tell your mom I said hey. She mentioned you two have dinner Friday — do not let her talk you into splitting the bread basket. She's been on a green day streak and I'd like to keep it going.

Good work over there.
— Dad

Lily,

Hey — following up on the 5:15 AM class situation at Buckhead. Coach Mike mentioned last Thursday that the slot might be getting bumped to 5:30 starting in July. That doesn't work for me. I'm out the door by 6:45 every morning to open the clinic, and 5:15 is the only window that lets me get a full session in, shower, and still hit my first patient on time.

I've been at that 5:15 class four days a week for going on three years. It's not a preference thing — it's a logistics thing. If the change is locked in, I need to know now so I can figure out alternatives. Can you pull up what's actually confirmed for the July schedule?

Appreciate it.
Brian Hall

Hi Brian!

So sorry for the confusion — I totally get why that's stressful, especially with your schedule. I pulled up the July programming calendar and I have good news: the 5:15 AM class is NOT moving to 5:30. What Coach Mike was referring to was a one-time shift on July 4th only, since the gym is opening late for the holiday. Every other weekday in July runs the normal 5:15 AM block.

You're confirmed active on the membership through August 31st, so no changes needed on your end. I'll flag your account with a note so you get an email heads-up if anything does shift in the future before it hits the floor.

Let me know if you need anything else!
Lily Hall
Member Support, CrossFit Buckhead

Lily,

Okay, that's a relief. July 4th I'm usually at Mom and Dad's in Marietta anyway, so that works out fine. Glad it was a miscommunication and not an actual schedule change — I was already doing the mental math on alternatives and none of them were good.

One more thing while I have you: can you confirm my guest pass count? I think I have two left on the account. Ethan's been asking about trying a class and I'd rather bring him in on a guest pass before he commits to anything. Kid's 16 and thinks he's ready for a 5:15 AM WOD. We'll see about that.

Thanks for getting back to me fast.
Brian

Brian,

Ha — I love that! The 5:15 AM class will definitely sort out whether he's serious. Good luck to him.

You actually have THREE guest passes remaining, not two — one was added when you renewed your annual membership back in March. All three are valid through your renewal date of March 14, 2027, and they can be used for any class including the 5:15 AM slot. Just bring Ethan to the front desk and we'll get him checked in and waivered up before class starts.

Coach Daniels runs the 5:15 on Tuesdays and Thursdays and she's great with first-timers — might be worth timing his first visit on one of those days. Hope he loves it!

Take care, Brian!
Lily Hall
Member Support, CrossFit Buckhead

Hey Lily,

Quick one. Missed my 5:15 AM class at CrossFit Buckhead this morning — app showed me as checked in but coach has me marked absent. Third time this month the system's glitched on me. Not a big deal today since it was a light skill day, but I've got a training block coming up and I need my attendance tracking clean.

Can you pull up my account and sort this out? Name's Brian Hall, email on file should be bhall@peakperformancept.com. Just need the record corrected and a heads-up if there's something on my end causing the sync issue.

Appreciate it.
Brian

Hi Brian,

Thanks for reaching out — got you pulled up right now. I can see the check-in attempt did register on our end at 5:13 AM, but there was a sync error between the app and our coaching dashboard. Your attendance record has been corrected and the absent mark is cleared. You're all good.

The issue was a known bug with our iOS app version 4.2.1 — it's affecting a small group of early morning check-ins. We pushed a fix yesterday, so updating your app should stop this from happening again. If it glitches again during your training block just shoot us a message and we'll fix it same day.

You're at 22 classes logged this month — solid work. Let me know if there's anything else I can help with!

Best,
Lily
CrossFit Buckhead Member Support

Hey Lily,

Following up on the Gmail API integration we discussed last week. Still not seeing the OAuth tokens refresh correctly on the clinic's Google Workspace account. Read/write scope is set, credentials are in place — but the connection drops after about an hour. That's a real problem when the whole point is automating appointment reminder emails.

I need this locked in before Monday. Coming off the holiday weekend we've got a packed schedule and the front desk can't be manually sending 40-plus reminders. That's not a sustainable play.

What do I need to do on my end to push this across the finish line?

Brian Hall
Peak Performance PT

Hi Brian,

Thanks for the follow-up — glad you circled back. I dug into the OAuth flow and found the culprit: your refresh token isn't being stored persistently because the app is still sitting in 'Testing' mode in Google Cloud Console. In testing mode, tokens expire after 7 days and won't auto-renew, which explains the hourly drops you're seeing.

Fix is straightforward. Go to Google Cloud Console → OAuth consent screen → change publishing status from 'Testing' to 'In production.' Once that's saved, re-authorize the app and your refresh tokens will persist indefinitely.

Let me know the moment you hit any friction on the re-auth step — I'll walk you through it in real time if needed.

Lily Hall
Support Team

Lily,

Done. Switched to production, re-authorized, ran a test batch of ten reminder emails — all delivered clean. Connection held well past the one-hour mark. That's exactly the PR we needed going into Monday.

Appreciate the fast turnaround. Front desk is set.

One follow-up question — is there a clean way to set rate limiting so we don't accidentally blow past the Gmail API daily send quota? Clinic volume isn't massive, but I don't want to be flying blind on that. Let me know what you'd recommend.

Brian Hall
Peak Performance PT

Hey Lily,

Following up on the Google Calendar API integration we talked about last week. Still hitting the same wall — appointment blocks I create in the clinic system aren't syncing to Google Calendar in real time. At Peak Performance PT I run back-to-back patient evals with zero buffer, so a 15-minute lag in calendar updates creates real scheduling problems. Had a double-book situation on Thursday because of it.

Credentials are confirmed working. Ran through the OAuth token refresh manually and that's not the issue. Something upstream is breaking the sync. Can you pull the ticket and tell me exactly where we are?

Brian Hall
Peak Performance PT | Atlanta

Hi Brian,

Thanks for following up — I pulled the ticket right away. Found the culprit: the push notification channel registered via the calendar.events.watch endpoint was returning a 200 but the channel expiration TTL was set far too short. Sync was dropping silently after the initial window closed, which explains the lag you were seeing.

I've pushed a fix to extend the TTL and wired in an auto-renewal handler so it won't expire going forward. The update should be live within the hour. When you get a chance, create a test appointment block and let me know if it appears on Google Calendar within 2 minutes. That'll confirm the fix is holding.

Here if you need anything else.

Lily

Lily,

Tested it first thing this morning. Created a 7 AM eval block right after I got back from CrossFit and it synced in under 90 seconds. That's exactly the performance we need — good fix.

One follow-up while I have you: I want a recurring admin block every weekday 12–1 PM that shows as busy in both the clinic system and Google. When I create recurring events through the API they're only showing up as single instances on the Google side — recurrence isn't carrying over. Known issue or something in my setup?

Email works best for this thread, easier to track.

Brian

Hi Brian,

Great news on the sync — 90 seconds is well within range, glad it's holding.

On the recurring events: yes, known behavior. The clinic system's API bridge is serializing recurring events as individual event objects instead of passing the RRULE string that Google Calendar expects. Without that RRULE in the recurrence array, Google treats each instance as a standalone event and your series never forms.

Fix is straightforward — I'll update the event serializer to include the recurrence field in the request body. Your 12–1 PM admin block will then land as a true recurring series on both ends. I'll have a patch in staging by end of day tomorrow and will ping you to test before we push to prod.

Lily

Lily,

Following up on the Google Drive API issue from last week. Still getting the 403 on the service account when the clinic's shared drive tries to pull the patient intake folder. It's blocking our front desk workflow and Sarah's had to manual-download every morning — not sustainable.

We're running the Drive API v3 with domain-wide delegation enabled. Checked the scopes: drive.readonly is listed. Still bouncing. I need eyes on whether the service account has the right impersonation setup in the Admin Console.

Been slammed — early morning CrossFit then back-to-back patients through 6 PM all week. Appreciate you staying on this. Need it squared away before end of week.

Brian

Dad,

Got it — sounds like a classic impersonation gap. Even with domain-wide delegation turned on, the service account still needs to be explicitly granted access inside Google Admin > Security > API Controls > Domain-wide Delegation. The drive.readonly scope has to be listed there exactly as written, not just in the GCP console.

Can you have Sarah confirm the client_email from the service account JSON and forward it to me? I'll cross-check it against what's authorized in Admin. Nine times out of ten that's the mismatch.

Also double-check the shared drive's membership — the service account (or the impersonated user) needs at least Viewer access on the shared drive itself, not just the folder. Both have to be true or it throws a 403.

Send me that client email and I'll get it resolved today. You shouldn't be dealing with manual downloads going into the weekend.

Lily

Lily,

Following up on the Notion API thread from last week. Got the integration token set up on my end but the database query keeps returning an empty array. I'm pulling patient scheduling blocks into a Notion board for the clinic and it's just not connecting.

Tried it before my 5:15 class this morning — same result. Ran the GET request, got a 200 back, but the results field is empty. Database ID looks right to me but I'm not a dev, so I could be wrong.

Can you look at the setup I sent over and tell me where I'm dropping the ball? Need this running before Thursday.

Brian

Hi Brian,

Thanks for the follow-up! I took a look at the config you shared and found the issue — the database ID you're using has the dashes stripped out. Notion's API requires the full hyphenated UUID format (xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx). That's why you're getting a 200 with empty results instead of an error; it's technically a valid request, just pointing nowhere.

Also double-check that your integration has been explicitly shared with that database inside Notion itself. Even with a valid token, if the integration isn't connected to the specific page, it won't return data. Go to the database → Share → invite your integration by name.

Let me know if that fixes it! Happy to jump on a quick call if you're still stuck after Thursday.

Lily

Lily,

That was exactly it. Fixed the UUID format, shared the integration with the database, ran the query — results came back clean. Took me ten minutes before the clinic opened this morning. Good rep.

Got one more thing. I want to filter the results by a property called 'Status' — only pull rows marked Active. Looked at the filter syntax in the docs and it's not clicking for me. Can you send me a working example for a select property filter?

Appreciate you tracking this down fast.

Brian

Hey Lily,

Following up on our conversation last week about the morning stack. Wanted to make sure you logged the updated protocol on your end.

Here's where I landed: Chemex black coffee first thing, then the smoothie — whey, banana, spinach, almond butter, creatine. Vitamin D is staying at 4000 IU and I kept the fish oil in. Been running this sequence for three weeks now and the 5:15 AM CrossFit sessions at Buckhead are feeling dialed in. Recovery is tracking better than Q1.

Main question: is there anything in your system flagging the creatine + fish oil combo timing-wise? I'm taking both pre-workout right now. Just want to make sure I'm not leaving anything on the table.

Thanks,
Dad

Hi Dad,

Got your note — glad the routine is clicking. I've reviewed the protocol you outlined and everything looks solid from a support standpoint.

On the creatine and fish oil timing: no flag on combining them, but fish oil is generally better absorbed with a meal due to its fat-soluble nature. Since your smoothie has almond butter in it, you're already in good shape — the fat content will help uptake. Taking both with the smoothie rather than standalone is the move.

One thing worth noting: if you ever scale back the banana (lower sugar day, whatever), the smoothie still gives you enough of a vehicle for the fish oil. No issues there.

Let me know if you want me to update the file with the 4000 IU vitamin D as the confirmed daily dose.

Lily

Lily,

Update the file — 4000 IU confirmed, non-negotiable. That's been the baseline for two years and I'm not moving off it.

Good call on the fish oil timing. I'd been second-guessing myself on that. Almond butter in the smoothie covers it, makes sense. Green light, moving on.

One more thing: Karen mentioned she saw something about fish oil quality tiers — molecular distillation vs. standard. Are we carrying anything in that tier or is that outside your scope? She's a pharmacist so when she flags something I take it seriously. Just want to know if it's worth a product swap or if what I'm using is already in that range.

Quick answer is fine.

Brian

Hi Brian,

File updated — Vitamin D 4000 IU locked in.

Karen's right to flag it. Molecular distillation is the gold standard — it strips heavy metals and oxidation byproducts that show up in lower-grade fish oil. The difference is real, especially at daily use over years. If you want to send me the brand you're currently using, I can check it against our quality benchmarks and give you a straight answer on whether a swap makes sense.

If you don't have the bottle handy, a good rule of thumb: look for a triglyceride form (not ethyl ester) with third-party testing on the label. That filters out most of the junk quickly.

Tell Karen she called it. And tell Ethan I said hey.

Lily

Hey Lily,

Quick one. Missed my 5:15 AM class at CrossFit Buckhead this morning — app showed me as checked in but coach has me marked absent. Third time this month the system's glitched on me. Not a big deal today since it was a light skill day, but I've got a training block coming up and I need my attendance tracking clean.

Can you pull up my account and sort this out? Name's Brian Hall, email on file should be bhall@peakperformancept.com. Just need the record corrected and a heads-up if there's something on my end causing the sync issue.

Appreciate it.
Brian

Hi Brian,

Thanks for reaching out — got you pulled up right now. I can see the check-in attempt did register on our end at 5:13 AM, but there was a sync error between the app and our coaching dashboard. Your attendance record has been corrected and the absent mark is cleared. You're all good.

The issue was a known bug with our iOS app version 4.2.1 — it's affecting a small group of early morning check-ins. We pushed a fix yesterday, so updating your app should stop this from happening again. If it glitches again during your training block just shoot us a message and we'll fix it same day.

You're at 22 classes logged this month — solid work. Let me know if there's anything else I can help with!

Best,
Lily
CrossFit Buckhead Member Support

Appreciate it.
— Dad (Brian Hall, Member #4421)

I'll text you once facilities confirms the patch is live. Don't let a sticky door reader mess up your clean day!

— Lily | CrossFit Buckhead Member Support

Lily,

Good. That's exactly what I needed — clean answer, fast. Backup code is saved.

Thursday is a heavy clean & jerk day, so that patch better hold. I'll test the reader Wednesday morning and use the code if it acts up. If it's still glitching after the rollback, I'll loop back to you directly.

Tell your mom I said hey. She mentioned you two have dinner Friday — do not let her talk you into splitting the bread basket. She's been on a green day streak and I'd like to keep it going.

Good work over there.
— Dad

I'm out the door by 6:45 every morning to open the clinic, and 5:15 is the only window that lets me get a full session in, shower, and still hit my first patient on time.

I've been at that 5:15 class four days a week for going on three years. It's not a preference thing — it's a logistics thing. If the change is locked in, I need to know now so I can figure out alternatives. Can you pull up what's actually confirmed for the July schedule?

Brian Hall

Hi Brian!

Let me know if you need anything else!
Lily Hall
Member Support, CrossFit Buckhead

Lily,

Okay, that's a relief. July 4th I'm usually at Mom and Dad's in Marietta anyway, so that works out fine. Glad it was a miscommunication and not an actual schedule change — I was already doing the mental math on alternatives and none of them were good.

One more thing while I have you: can you confirm my guest pass count? I think I have two left on the account. Ethan's been asking about trying a class and I'd rather bring him in on a guest pass before he commits to anything. Kid's 16 and thinks he's ready for a 5:15 AM WOD. We'll see about that.

Thanks for getting back to me fast.
Brian

Brian,

Ha — I love that! The 5:15 AM class will definitely sort out whether he's serious. Good luck to him.

You actually have THREE guest passes remaining, not two — one was added when you renewed your annual membership back in March. All three are valid through your renewal date of March 14, 2027, and they can be used for any class including the 5:15 AM slot. Just bring Ethan to the front desk and we'll get him checked in and waivered up before class starts.

Coach Daniels runs the 5:15 on Tuesdays and Thursdays and she's great with first-timers — might be worth timing his first visit on one of those days. Hope he loves it!

Take care, Brian!
Lily Hall
Member Support, CrossFit Buckhead
